caption:
4 ways to stream LLM responses in TypeScript.

SSE, WebSocket, ReadableStream, async iterators — each has a sweet spot. Pick the wrong one and your users stare at a spinner for 10 seconds.

📌 SAVE this streaming reference.

Which pattern do you use? 👇

#typescript #llm #ai #streaming #webdev #coding #programming #developer #openai #codingtips

slides.title: Server-Sent Events — the simplest
slides.desc: One-directional stream over HTTP. Works with fetch. No library needed.
slides.punchline: Best for: most LLM chat UIs.

slides.title: WebSocket — bidirectional
slides.desc: Full-duplex. Send and receive simultaneously. Persistent connection.

slides.title: ReadableStream — transform on the fly
slides.desc: Chain transforms. Parse JSON chunks. Backpressure built in.

slides.title: Async iterators — the cleanest
slides.desc: for-await-of over a stream. Reads like synchronous code.

slides.title: LLM Streaming Cheat Sheet
slides.question: Which streaming pattern do you use? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: Web Workers — move heavy computation off the main thread
